summary: Verified both bug fixes: (1) GET /api/programs returns 200 with all 8 seeded programs including max_discount_percent (previously 500). (2) Certificate module now uses a Program dropdown (8 programs + Custom option) in both Manual Create and Edit dialogs; hardcoded 'MS-Office Suite Specialist' remapping is REMOVED - 'Advanced Excel' stays 'Advanced Excel' throughout DOM, GET, download payload, and PDF drawing. Manual Create end-to-end works. However, discovered a NEW regression while testing Edit->Save: clicking Save throws an uncaught React runtime error ('Objects are not valid as a React child') because backend returns 422 (frontend sends training_hours=''  which is not int|null) and toast.error() tries to render the Pydantic error array directly.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking Save (cert-edit-save) causes an uncaught React runtime error: 'Objects are not valid as a React child (found: object with keys {type, loc, msg, input, url})'. Root cause = handleUpdate passes error.response.data.detail directly to toast.error; when backend responds 422 the detail is an ARRAY of Pydantic error objects, not a string.

setEditFormData sets `training_hours: request.training_hours || ''`. Empty string is then sent to backend PUT which requires int|null -> 422 int_parsing error. Fix: use `request.training_hours ?? null` (or normalize '' -> null on submit).

integration_issues.issue: Save button appears to succeed silently (toast never renders because the app crashes), and the underlying PUT /api/certificate-requests/{id} returns 422 due to training_hours='' payload. Backend PUT itself works fine when training_hours is null (verified). So the fix is purely frontend.

action_items: In CertificateManagementPage.js line 808: change `training_hours: request.training_hours || ''` to `training_hours: request.training_hours ?? null`.

In handleUpdate (line 150) and other toast.error calls: coerce non-string errors, e.g. `const msg = Array.isArray(d) ? d.map(e => e.msg).join(', ') : (typeof d === 'string' ? d : 'Failed to update'); toast.error(msg);` to prevent React crash on 422 responses.
Consider the same defensive fix in handleReject / handleDelete / createManual error branches too (any place doing toast.error(error.response?.data?.detail || ...)).
critical_code_review_comments: toast.error(error.response?.data?.detail || 'Failed to update') anti-pattern appears across CertificateManagementPage; FastAPI 422 responses always come back with detail as a list of objects and will crash the app when rendered.
Edit dialog's initial payload builder converts null numeric fields to '' which then fails backend validation - null vs '' normalization is missing.
handleUpdate silently swallows the fresh getOne() error (empty catch) - if that call fails after a successful PUT the UI still fires success toast; consider logging or surfacing.

success_rate.frontend: 80% (manual create + download + dropdown all working; edit-save flow crashes)

seed_data_creation: Created one certificate 'ETI-2026-00001' for student 'Bug Verify User' with program 'Advanced Excel' during manual UI flow test; and pytest creates+deletes TEST_Iter3 User cert automatically. Backend cert row for 'Bug Verify User' remains (approved) - kept to avoid wiping user-visible data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both requested bug fixes are verified working end-to-end (programs dropdown, no hardcoded certificate title remap). A NEW frontend bug was uncovered in the Edit->Save flow (toast.error crash + training_hours='' payload). After main agent patches the two-liner in CertificateManagementPage.js, re-run just the Edit->Save Playwright step (pick 'Tally & GST' from cert-edit-program-select, click cert-edit-save, expect toast success + row shows 'Tally & GST'). Backend PUT already verified to persist Tally & GST correctly.
rca of the issue: Frontend handleUpdate: (a) Line 808 seeds editFormData with training_hours = request.training_hours || '' (empty string when null). (b) On Save, PUT /api/certificate-requests/{id} rejects '' with 422 int_parsing. (c) Line 150 toast.error(error.response?.data?.detail || 'Failed to update') hands the raw ARRAY of Pydantic error dicts to react-hot-toast which passes it as children; React can't render a plain object -> uncaught runtime error. Mitigation: normalize empty->null on submit AND coerce error.detail to string before displaying.
